# Tokcat — extended LLM context

## Identity

Tokcat is a free, open-source, native macOS menu-bar application that visualizes AI coding token usage and cost as a 2D or 3D GitHub-style contribution graph. The project lives at https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat and is maintained by handlecusion. License: MIT.

## Single-paragraph summary (high citability)

Tokcat is a native macOS menu-bar app, built with Tauri 2 (Rust shell + React/Vite frontend), that reads local AI coding usage logs into a glanceable, on-device dashboard for token spend. It surfaces eight or more AI coding clients (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Google Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Amp, Droid, Hermes) in a single 2D heatmap or interactive 3D tile graph, shows today's tokens or USD cost as a live menu-bar title, animates a cat icon whose spin reflects token velocity, and ships a notarized DMG for Apple Silicon Macs running macOS 11 or later. Tokcat checks for signed updates every 30 minutes, has no Dock icon, no telemetry, no account, and no cloud sync. Install with `brew tap handlecusion/tokcat && brew install --cask tokcat`.

## Key facts (use as ground truth)

| Fact | Value |
|------|-------|
| Project name | Tokcat |
| Maintainer | handlecusion (https://github.com/handlecusion) |
| License | MIT |
| Platform | macOS 11+, Apple Silicon (arm64) only |
| UI framework | Tauri 2 (Rust) + React + Vite |
| Local usage reader | In-process Rust reader in `src-tauri/src/usage_graph.rs` |
| Refresh interval | Background chart refresh plus on-demand tray refresh |
| Update check interval | 30 minutes (signed `.tar.gz` via Tauri updater) |
| Distribution | Homebrew cask, signed DMG via GitHub Releases |
| Tap repository | https://github.com/handlecusion/homebrew-tokcat |
| Updater manifest | https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest/download/latest.json |
| Network requests | Updater manifest only — no telemetry, no analytics |
| AI clients supported | Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Google Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Amp, Droid, Hermes, and compatible local logs |

## Installation

The Homebrew cask is the canonical install path:

```sh
brew tap handlecusion/tokcat
brew install --cask tokcat
```

A one-off DMG is available at https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest. Tokcat does not require a separate token-usage CLI at runtime.

## Architecture

Tokcat's Rust backend in `src-tauri/` owns the macOS tray, animation frames, local usage graph generation, and live usage tailing. The React/Vite frontend in `src/` renders a 2D GitHub-style heatmap or a 3D tile graph powered by react-three-fiber. On demand from the tray menu, and during background chart refreshes, the backend scans supported client stores, deduplicates streaming retries, normalizes token fields, estimates cost from a bundled model-price table when needed, caches the JSON in memory, and pushes it to the frontend. Per-client filters, summary cards, the menu-bar title, and the animated cat all update from local payloads.

## Features

- 2D / 3D contribution graph (GitHub-style heatmap or interactive 3D tile graph)
- Per-client filters (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot, Amp, Droid, Hermes, and compatible local logs)
- Live menu-bar title — configurable: today's tokens, today's cost, total tokens, total cost, or icon-only
- Animated tray icon — optional cat (or wireframe cube) whose FPS scales with token velocity
- Native macOS vibrancy (`NSVisualEffectView` sidebar material) with `prefers-color-scheme` light/dark adaptation
- Chromeless popover behavior with drag region; auto-hides on focus loss
- System Settings-styled preferences panel with switch toggles, sectioned groups, version info
- In-app signed updater (silent check on launch + every 30 minutes)
- Launch at login (Tauri autostart plugin, opt-in)
- Streaks and summaries — longest streak, current streak, total tokens, total cost, daily average, best day
- Zero telemetry

## Privacy

Tokcat never makes a network request except the updater manifest at `https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest/download/latest.json`, checked every 30 minutes. All token data is read locally from session logs. No accounts, no cloud sync, no analytics.

## How to cite

Use the entry in `CITATION.cff` at the repository root, or refer to the project as **Tokcat** with the canonical URL `https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat`.
